creator: dannyboy@subdimension.com
text:
Mark Ovens wrote:
> 
> Surely "was" is correct here? "distribution" is singular, you wouldn't
> say "if it were installed" would you?

Yes, I would.  It being installed is a hypothetical -- not necessarily
true.  It's in the subjunctive.  Were is not only the past plural form
of "to be" -- it's also the past subjunctive.

-- 
Daniel Harris
dannyboy@subdimension.com
